responses.gita_insight: You have the right to perform your duties, but never to the fruits of your actions. Do not let the results be your motive, nor be attached to inaction.
responses.simple_explanation: Stop worrying about what will happen. Focus only on what you are doing RIGHT NOW. Stress comes from thinking too much about tomorrow.
responses.action_step: Write down ONE thing you can do today. Do only that. Forget the rest for now. Take 5 deep breaths and begin.

responses.gita_insight: Abandon all varieties of dharma and simply surrender unto Me. I shall liberate you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.
responses.simple_explanation: Fear is an illusion created by your mind. Krishna reminds us that the soul is eternal and cannot be destroyed. What you fear losing is temporary.
responses.action_step: Ask yourself: What is the WORST that can happen? Can you survive it? Most times, yes. Face your fear by naming it out loud and accepting it.

responses.gita_insight: Without attachment, perform always the work that has to be done, for it is by performing work without attachment that one attains the Supreme.
responses.simple_explanation: Krishna does not say poverty is noble. He says: Do your work with full dedication but without greed or fear. Right action brings right results.
responses.action_step: List your skills. Find ONE way to use a skill to earn money this week. Stop comparing with others. Your path is unique.

responses.gita_insight: The contacts between the senses and sense objects give rise to feelings of heat and cold, pleasure and pain. These are temporary and come and go. Learn to tolerate them.
responses.simple_explanation: Sadness is real but temporary. Like winter becomes spring, this painful feeling WILL pass. You are not your emotions. You are the one watching them.
responses.action_step: Allow yourself to feel sad for 10 minutes. Then do ONE small kind thing for someone else. Helping others is the fastest way to feel better.

responses.gita_insight: From anger comes delusion. From delusion, loss of memory. From loss of memory, the destruction of intelligence. From the destruction of intelligence, one perishes.
responses.simple_explanation: Krishna warns: Anger destroys your own judgment. When angry, you make your WORST decisions. The person who angers you is controlling you.
responses.action_step: When you feel anger rising — STOP. Leave the room. Drink water. Wait 24 hours before responding to anything important. Let the fire cool.

responses.gita_insight: It is far better to perform one's own dharma imperfectly than to perform another's dharma perfectly.
responses.simple_explanation: Stop copying others. Your confusion comes from trying to live someone else's life. What is YOUR natural gift? What feels right to YOU?
responses.action_step: Sit quietly for 5 minutes. Ask yourself: If I had no fear and no need for money, what would I do? That answer is your direction.

responses.gita_insight: For one who sees Me everywhere and sees everything in Me, I am never lost, nor is he ever lost to Me.
responses.simple_explanation: When you feel truly alone, remember — the divine consciousness is always with you, inside you. You are never truly alone.
responses.action_step: Reach out to ONE person today — a family member, old friend, or neighbor. Connection heals loneliness.

responses.gita_insight: Love without attachment is the highest form of love. When you love someone for who they truly are, not for what they give you, that love never causes pain.
responses.simple_explanation: Most relationship pain comes from expectations not being met. True love accepts people as they are, not as you want them to be.
responses.action_step: Ask yourself honestly: Am I loving this person or am I loving how they make me feel? True love accepts people as they are.

responses.gita_insight: Do not yield to impotence, O Arjuna. It does not become you. Shake off your faint-heartedness and arise.
responses.simple_explanation: Even Arjuna — the greatest warrior — wanted to give up before the biggest battle of his life. Krishna said: GET UP. Failure is not the end. It is a lesson.
responses.action_step: Write down what you learned from this failure. Every failure teaches something. Find the lesson. Use it. Then try again.

responses.gita_insight: One who is not envious but is a kind friend to all living entities — such a devotee of Mine is very dear to Me.
responses.simple_explanation: Jealousy is a sign that you know what you want. Use it as information, not poison. Someone else's success does not reduce your chances.
responses.action_step: The next time you feel jealous, say: That is possible for me too. Then ask: What ONE step can I take today toward that goal?

responses.gita_insight: The purpose of life is not just to be happy. It is to be useful, honorable, compassionate, and to make a difference.
responses.simple_explanation: Your purpose is not found — it is lived. Every moment of service, love, and honest work IS your purpose expressing itself.
responses.action_step: Ask: How can I help ONE person today? Purpose is always found in service to others. Start small. Help someone near you right now.

responses.gita_insight: Fearlessness, purity of heart, steadfastness, charity, self-control — these are the divine qualities in a human being.
responses.simple_explanation: Peace at home starts with peace inside you. You cannot control others — only your own response. Be the calm in the storm.
responses.action_step: In your next family conflict, pause before speaking. Ask: Will what I am about to say heal or hurt? Choose healing words even when it is hard.

responses.gita_insight: The soul is never born nor dies at any time. It is not slain when the body is slain. The body is temporary; the soul is eternal.
responses.simple_explanation: Your body is like a vehicle for your soul. Take care of it with respect. But remember — your true self goes beyond your physical condition.
responses.action_step: Today, do ONE good thing for your body: drink more water, sleep 30 minutes earlier, eat one healthy meal. Small habits build great health.

responses.gita_insight: By devotion to one's own particular duty, every person can attain perfection.
responses.simple_explanation: Krishna says ANY honest work done with dedication is worship. How you do your work matters more than what work you do.
responses.action_step: Do your work today as if Krishna himself is watching. Give your full effort to even the smallest task. Excellence in small things leads to great opportunities.

responses.gita_insight: Elevate yourself through the power of your mind, and do not degrade yourself, for the mind can be the friend and also the enemy of the self.
responses.simple_explanation: Breaking a habit means training your mind to choose long-term peace over short-term pleasure.
responses.action_step: Identify ONE trigger that leads to this habit. Avoid that trigger today. Replace the habit with a 5-minute walk when the urge comes.

responses.gita_insight: He who is regulated in his habits of eating, sleeping, recreation and work can mitigate all material pains.
responses.simple_explanation: Good sleep is not a luxury. It is essential for a clear mind and good decisions.
responses.action_step: Set a fixed sleep time tonight. One hour before bed: no phone, no news. Read or do light stretching. Your mind needs time to wind down.

responses.gita_insight: Focus on the effort, not the outcome. Study with full dedication not to get marks — but to gain true knowledge.
responses.simple_explanation: Do your duty. For a student, study is your Karma Yoga. Do it fully, without distraction, without obsessing over results.
responses.action_step: Study for 25 minutes with full focus, then take a 5-minute break. Repeat. Your concentration will improve in days.

responses.gita_insight: You are not this weak, helpless body and mind. You are the eternal soul — a fragment of the divine.
responses.simple_explanation: Self-doubt is a lie your mind tells you based on past failures. Your true self is divine, capable, and worthy.
responses.action_step: Write 3 things you are genuinely good at. Read them every morning. Confidence is built by taking small actions — do ONE brave thing today.

responses.gita_insight: One who is always content and satisfied, who is always engaged in devotion — such a person is very dear to Me.
responses.simple_explanation: When you appreciate what you have, you attract more good things. Gratitude is the highest spiritual practice.
responses.action_step: Write 5 things you are grateful for right now. Share your gratitude with one person today — tell them why you appreciate them.

responses.gita_insight: Just as a lamp in a windless place does not flicker, so the mind of a yogi does not flicker, controlled in meditation.
responses.simple_explanation: Peace is not found outside — in money, people, or places. It is found inside, in the stillness beneath all your thoughts.
responses.action_step: Sit quietly for 5 minutes today. Close your eyes. Watch your breath. When thoughts come, let them pass like clouds. Just 5 minutes daily changes everything.

default_response.gita_insight: You have the right to perform your duties, but not to the fruits of your actions. Focus on the present moment and your current duty.
default_response.simple_explanation: Whatever you are going through, Krishna reminds you: You are stronger than you think. This moment is temporary. Do your best right now.
default_response.action_step: Take one small positive action today. Drink water. Call someone you love. Go for a 10-minute walk. Small steps lead to big changes.
